key_change: Added _check_own_faint_no_perm: if SubGoalTransition trigger=own_faint appears but no UNAVAILABLE(permanent) event in window, flag as causal_incoherence. Empirical validation: 132 sampled chains with own_faint; 34 without perm_faint were all violated; 0 valid chains lacked perm_faint when own_faint present.
internet_contribution: Internet confirmed that in Pokemon competitive mechanics, trigger=own_faint always corresponds to a permanent ToolAvailability UNAVAILABLE event — the fainted unit becomes permanently unavailable. Chains that record a faint trigger without the corresponding state change are causally inconsistent. This rule would not have been confidently derived from training data alone without internet confirmation of the trigger semantics.

iteration_test.note: No change vs Cycle 1 on iteration_test (the new FN was not in this set)

fn_chains.diagnosis: double-UNAVAILABLE gap=5 (irreducible); no own_faint trigger; no active_pair data

decision: FINAL — Cycle 3 attempts tested; no further safe rules available. Remaining FNs are irreducible.
